fields.definition: Large areas of land covered densely with trees and undergrowth.

fields.definition: To extend over or spread across a surface or area.
fields.example: Dark clouds began to cover the sky before the storm.

fields.definition: Approximately; used to express an estimate or rough figure.
fields.example: The hike takes about three hours to complete.

fields.definition: The number 1; a single unit or entity.
fields.example: One tree can absorb several kilograms of carbon dioxide per year.

fields.definition: Constituting number three in a sequence; one of three equal parts.
fields.example: A third of the planet's fresh water is locked in glaciers.

fields.definition: The planet on which we live; the world.

fields.definition: The solid part of the Earth's surface as distinct from sea or water.

fields.definition: The outermost or uppermost layer of something.

fields.definition: Second person singular present and first, second, third person plural of 'be'.

fields.definition: The natural environment or habitat of an animal or plant.

fields.definition: A greater or additional amount or degree.

fields.definition: The Earth together with all its countries and peoples.

fields.definition: Of, on, or relating to the Earth or dry land (as opposed to water or air).

fields.definition: Used to refer to two or more people or things previously mentioned.

fields.definition: To control or maintain something at a desired level.

fields.definition: The long-term pattern of weather in a particular area.

fields.definition: Through the means or agency of; performed by.

fields.definition: Taking in or soaking up energy, liquid, or another substance.

fields.definition: A colorless, odorless gas that most living organisms need to breathe.

fields.definition: A sheet, coating, or thickness of material covering a surface.

fields.definition: Things that are very large in size or importance.

fields.usage: Refers to an individual who creates literary works in verse
examples: The poet recited his latest work.

fields.definition: To stop something from happening or arising
fields.example: We must prevent pollution.
fields.usage: Used to describe actions taken to stop an event or process

fields.definition: Occupational fields requiring specialized training and formal qualifications
fields.example: Medical professions
fields.usage: Refers to different career paths and professional disciplines

fields.definition: Showing a lack of courage or determination; timid
fields.example: A pusillanimous leader
fields.usage: Describes someone who is extremely cowardly or spineless

fields.definition: People who read books, texts, or publications
fields.example: Avid readers
fields.usage: Refers to individuals who consume written content

fields.definition: The back part of something; to raise or breed
fields.example: The rear of the building
fields.usage: Can be used as a noun or verb with multiple meanings

fields.definition: Cease work or movement in order to relax; remainder
fields.example: Take a rest
fields.usage: Can be used as a noun or verb with different contexts

fields.definition: Solid mineral materials of the earth's crust; to move back and forth
fields.example: Geological rocks
fields.usage: Can be a noun or verb with different meanings

fields.definition: Referring to a previously mentioned person or thing
fields.example: The said document
fields.usage: Past tense of 'say', often used in formal writing

fields.definition: Large bodies of salt water connecting continents
fields.example: Mediterranean seas
fields.usage: Refers to oceanic regions and marine environments

fields.definition: Information known only to a select few
fields.example: Government secrets
fields.usage: Refers to confidential or hidden information

fields.definition: Past tense of 'seem', indicating an appearance or perception
fields.example: He seemed tired after the long journey.
fields.usage: Used to describe how something appeared to be or was perceived
examples: He seemed tired after the long journey.
It seemed like a good idea at the time.

fields.definition: The ability to appreciate or be sensitive to feelings, emotions, or aesthetic qualities
fields.example: She had a keen sensibility for art.
fields.usage: Refers to emotional understanding or refined perception
examples: She had a keen sensibility for art.
His sensibility was deeply affected by the music.

fields.definition: Made of or resembling silk; smooth and soft
fields.example: She wore a silken scarf.
fields.usage: Describes texture or quality similar to silk

fields.definition: Present tense of 'sit', to be in a seated position
fields.example: She sits by the window.
fields.usage: Describes the act of resting on a seat or surface
examples: The cat sits on the chair.

fields.definition: Of limited size or amount; not large
fields.example: A small room.
fields.usage: Describes something of reduced dimensions or significance

fields.definition: Moving by taking steps; progressing
fields.example: He is stepping carefully.
fields.usage: Describes the action of walking or moving forward

fields.definition: Academic pursuit of knowledge; systematic investigation
fields.example: He studies mathematics.
fields.usage: Refers to academic research or learning activities
examples: Her studies focus on climate change.

fields.definition: Lasting for a limited time; not permanent
fields.example: A temporary job.
fields.usage: Describes something that exists or is true for a short period

fields.definition: Possessive pronoun indicating ownership by a group
fields.example: Their house is beautiful.
fields.usage: Used to show possession or association with a group

fields.definition: At that time; next in sequence
fields.example: First we'll eat, then we'll go.
fields.usage: Used to indicate a subsequent point in time or sequence
examples: First we'll eat, then we'll go.

fields.definition: An object, item, or entity that is not specifically named
fields.example: Pass me that thing on the table.
fields.usage: Used to refer to an unspecified or generic object
examples: Pass me that thing on the table.
It's a strange thing to see.

fields.definition: To form or have a mental opinion or idea
fields.example: I think it might rain.
fields.usage: Used to express mental consideration or belief
examples: What do you think about this?

fields.definition: Used to introduce a contrasting statement or concession
fields.example: It was expensive, though worth it.
fields.usage: Indicates a contrast or qualification to a previous statement
examples: It was expensive, though worth it.

fields.definition: Thin strands of twisted fiber used in weaving or sewing
fields.example: Silk threads were used in the embroidery.
fields.usage: Refers to textile fibers or clothing in informal context
examples: Silk threads were used in the embroidery.

fields.definition: Periods or instances of occurrence
fields.example: Many times I've told you.
fields.usage: Indicates frequency or specific moments

fields.definition: Slightly colored or flavored with a quality or emotion
fields.example: His voice was tinged with sadness.
fields.usage: Describes a subtle coloration or influence
examples: His voice was tinged with sadness.
The sky was tinged with pink.

fields.definition: The current day or present time period
fields.example: Today is Monday.
fields.usage: Refers to the present day or contemporary era

fields.definition: Lasting only for a short time; impermanent
fields.example: Transient emotions quickly pass.
fields.usage: Describes something temporary or fleeting

fields.definition: Trembling or quivering, often from emotion
fields.example: Her tremulous voice betrayed her nervousness.
fields.usage: Describes a shaky or uncertain state
examples: Her tremulous voice betrayed her nervousness.

fields.definition: Lacking skill or experience in a particular activity
fields.example: An unpractised musician.
fields.usage: Describes someone who is inexperienced or untrained
examples: She was unpractised in public speaking.

fields.definition: Toward or in a higher position or level
fields.example: Look up at the sky.
fields.usage: Directional adverb indicating upward movement or elevation

fields.definition: On or at; indicating position or location
fields.example: Sitting upon the chair.
fields.usage: Formal preposition indicating placement or occurrence

fields.definition: Past tense of wait; remained in place expecting something
fields.example: She waited for the bus.
fields.usage: Indicates completed action of waiting in the past

fields.definition: Large buildings for storing goods or merchandise
fields.example: Industrial warehouses store inventory.
fields.usage: Plural noun referring to storage facilities

fields.definition: Intricate networks of fine threads created by spiders
fields.example: Spider webs glistened in sunlight.
fields.usage: Plural noun describing interconnected structures

fields.definition: At what or which time; introducing a clause about timing
fields.example: When will you arrive?
fields.usage: Interrogative and relative adverb indicating time

fields.definition: In which; used to indicate a context or situation
fields.example: The contract wherein terms are specified.
fields.usage: Formal relative adverb specifying a condition or place
examples: The contract wherein terms are specified.

fields.definition: Used to refer to something previously mentioned
fields.example: The book, which was old, fascinated me.
fields.usage: Relative pronoun introducing a descriptive clause
examples: The book, which was old, fascinated me.

fields.definition: Expressing future action or intention
fields.example: I will call you later.
fields.usage: Modal verb indicating future events or willingness

fields.definition: Expressing conditional or hypothetical situations
fields.example: I would help if I could.
fields.usage: Modal verb indicating potential or imagined actions
examples: I would help if I could.

fields.definition: People who create written works such as books, articles, or stories
fields.example: Successful writers often spend years honing their craft
fields.usage: Refers to professionals or individuals who engage in writing as a profession or hobby
examples: Successful writers often spend years honing their craft
Many writers work from home or coffee shops

fields.definition: Second-person pronoun referring to the person or people being addressed
fields.example: You are welcome to join us
fields.usage: Used to directly address someone in singular or plural contexts
examples: You are welcome to join us
Can you help me with this?

fields.title: Forests
fields.text: Forests cover about one-third of the Earth's land surface and are home to more than half of the world's terrestrial species. They regulate the climate by absorbing carbon dioxide and releasing oxygen through the process of photosynthesis. The canopy layer, formed by the tallest trees, filters sunlight and creates a humid microclimate beneath it. Fallen leaves and decaying matter enrich the soil, sustaining a complex web of organisms from fungi to insects.

fields.title: Science
fields.text: The solar system consists of the Sun and the eight planets that orbit around it, along with moons, asteroids, and comets. Gravity is the invisible force that keeps every planet locked in its elliptical path around the Sun. The inner planets — Mercury, Venus, Earth, and Mars — are rocky and dense, while the outer planets are massive gas giants. Scientists continue to explore the outer edges of the solar system, where the Voyager probes have now traveled beyond the heliopause.

fields.title: Self-Reliance
fields.text: A foolish consistency is the hobgoblin of little minds, adored by little statesmen and philosophers and divines. With consistency a great soul has simply nothing to do. He may as well concern himself with his shadow on the wall. Speak what you think today in hard words, and tomorrow speak what tomorrow thinks in hard words again, though it contradict every thing you said today.

fields.title: Moby-Dick
fields.text: Whenever I find myself growing grim about the mouth; whenever it is a damp, drizzly November in my soul; whenever I find myself involuntarily pausing before coffin warehouses, and bringing up the rear of every funeral I meet; and especially whenever my hypos get such an upper hand of me, that it requires a strong moral principle to prevent me from deliberately stepping into the street, and methodically knocking people's hats off — then, I account it high time to get to sea as soon as I can.

fields.title: The Art of Fiction
fields.text: Experience is never limited, and it is never complete; it is an immense sensibility, a kind of huge spider-web of the finest silken threads suspended in the chamber of consciousness, and catching every air-borne particle in its tissue. It is the very atmosphere of the mind; and when the mind is imaginative — much more when it happens to be that of a man of genius — it takes to itself the faintest hints of life, it converts the very pulses of the air into revelations.

fields.title: The Renaissance
fields.text: She is older than the rocks among which she sits; like the vampire, she has been dead many times, and learned the secrets of the grave; and has been a diver in deep seas, and keeps their fallen day about her; and trafficked for strange webs with Eastern merchants; and, as Leda, was the mother of Helen of Troy, and, as Saint Anne, the mother of Mary; and all this has been to her but as the sound of lyres and flutes, and lives only in the delicacy with which it has moulded the changing lineaments, and tinged the eyelids and the hands.

fields.title: Preface to Shakespeare
fields.text: Shakespeare is, above all writers, at least above all modern writers, the poet of nature; the poet that holds up to his readers a faithful mirror of manners and of life. His characters are not modified by the customs of particular places, unpractised by the rest of the world; by the peculiarities of studies or professions, which can operate but upon small numbers; or by the accidents of transient fashions or temporary opinions: they are the genuine progeny of common humanity, such as the world will always supply, and observation will always find.

fields.title: Sartor Resartus
fields.text: I lived in a continual, indefinite, pining fear; tremulous, pusillanimous, apprehensive of I knew not what: it seemed as if all things in the Heavens above and the Earth beneath would hurt me; as if the Heavens and the Earth were but boundless jaws of a devouring monster, wherein I, palpitating, waited to be devoured.
